game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
OneShot is a surreal top down Puzzle/Adventure game with unique gameplay capabilities. You are to guide a child through a mysterious world on a mission to restore its long-dead sun. The world knows you exist.

OneShot: World Machine Edition

OneShot: World Machine Edition has made its way to Steam!

This new version places the game within a mock OS, the World Machine OS, allowing the game to be played not only on Windows PCs, but on Steam Deck as well.

It also features many new enhancements, such as upgraded graphics, new UI, full analog movement support, Character profile galleries, cutscene galleries, and much much more!


About the Game



A surreal puzzle adventure game with unique mechanics / capabilities.

You are to guide a child through a mysterious world on a mission to restore its long-dead sun.

...Of course, things are never that simple.

The world knows you exist.

The consequences are real.

Saving the world may be impossible.

You only have one shot.

FEATURES

  • Gameplay mechanics that go beyond the game window.
  • A haunting original soundtrack and artwork designed to match.
  • A unique relationship between a game and its player.
  • A lingering feeling that you're not getting the full story unless you know where to look.

CONTENT WARNING

Although OneShot is not a horror game in the traditional sense, parts of the game may induce some paranoia. Please proceed with caution.

CLOSING THE GAME WINDOW

Do not worry, it is safe to do so. It only saves your progress.
game_info.description: Guide Niko, a child with a glowing lightbulb, through a mysterious dying world to restore the Sun Machine. Navigate through increasingly complex puzzle rooms filled with obstacles, switches, and locked doors. The world is aware of your presence - switches remember their states, and your actions have consequences. Reach the Sun Chamber and place the lightbulb to restore light to this forsaken realm. You only have one shot at saving this world.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move Niko in four directions
Space: Interact with switches and doors
Z: Pick up/Put down the lightbulb
Shift: Sprint (moves faster)
Enter: Start game from main menu
Esc: Pause/Unpause game
R: Return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement mechanics and collision detection. Validates that the player can move in all four directions, collides properly with walls, and position tracking works correctly.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the player in all four cardinal directions systematically. Test collision with walls by attempting to walk into them. Verify the player stays within bounds and position updates correctly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully moves in all directions without passing through walls. Position data is accurately tracked in gameState. No errors occur during movement operations.

TEST_1.strategy: Move the player in all four cardinal directions systematically. Test collision with walls by attempting to walk into them. Verify the player stays within bounds and position updates correctly.
TEST_1.expected: Player successfully moves in all directions without passing through walls. Position data is accurately tracked in gameState. No errors occur during movement operations.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the optimal win strategy by solving all puzzles efficiently. This validates switch interactions, door unlocking mechanics, lightbulb carrying, and reaching the win condition at the Sun Chamber.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Navigate to each switch in an optimal order, activate them to unlock doors progressively. Pick up the lightbulb when available. Navigate through all unlocked rooms to reach the Sun Chamber. Place the lightbulb to win.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All switches are activated correctly, doors unlock in sequence, lightbulb is carried to the Sun Chamber, and GAME_OVER_WIN state is reached. The sun is restored and the world is saved.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests the sprint mechanic and validates that player can move faster when holding shift. Ensures stamina system works properly and movement speed changes are correctly implemented.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Toggle between normal walking and sprinting. Measure position changes over time to validate speed differences. Test stamina depletion and regeneration mechanics.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Player moves noticeably faster while sprinting. Stamina depletes during sprint and regenerates when not sprinting. Speed returns to normal when stamina is depleted.
TEST_3.description: Tests the sprint mechanic and validates that player can move faster when holding shift. Ensures stamina system works properly and movement speed changes are correctly implemented.
TEST_3.strategy: Toggle between normal walking and sprinting. Measure position changes over time to validate speed differences. Test stamina depletion and regeneration mechanics.
TEST_3.expected: Player moves noticeably faster while sprinting. Stamina depletes during sprint and regenerates when not sprinting. Speed returns to normal when stamina is depleted.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests interaction system with switches and doors. Validates that proximity-based interactions work, switches toggle states correctly, and doors respond appropriately.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Approach various switches and activate them using Space key. Verify visual feedback and state changes. Test door unlocking after correct switch combinations are activated.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Switches toggle correctly when player is nearby and presses Space. Doors unlock when required switches are activated. Visual and state feedback is accurate.

TEST_4.expected: Switches toggle correctly when player is nearby and presses Space. Doors unlock when required switches are activated. Visual and state feedback is accurate.

TEST_5.test_description: Tests the lightbulb pickup and carry mechanic. Validates that the player can only pick up the lightbulb when certain conditions are met and that carrying it affects gameplay appropriately.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Navigate to the lightbulb location after unlocking necessary doors. Press Z to pick it up. Carry it through various rooms to test if it's properly tracked. Place it at the Sun Chamber to win.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Lightbulb can be picked up and put down correctly. Visual indication shows when player is carrying it. Game tracks lightbulb state properly. Win condition triggers when placed at Sun Chamber.
